name: Plasmic Visual Page Builder and Headless CMS API
what_it_does: Visual page builder and headless CMS with REST API — enabling marketing teams and developers to collaboratively build pages using a Figma-like visual editor that works with real React/Next.js/Gatsby code components, delivering headless content via REST API, with design-to-code import from Figma, content management, and A/B testing.
use_cases: Delivering headless page content via Plasmic REST API for Next.js or React frontend integration
Enabling marketing teams to build and edit landing pages visually without developer involvement
Importing Figma designs to Plasmic as editable page components for rapid page creation
Managing content changes and scheduling publishes via Plasmic API for content automation
A/B testing page variants via Plasmic API for conversion rate optimization
not_for: Complex content management with approval workflows (Plasmic is page-building focused)
Enterprise DXP with deep personalization and marketing automation
best_when: A growth team needs to rapidly create and iterate landing pages within a React/Next.js app — with Figma-to-code import for design handoff and a visual editor for non-developers to make changes without PRs.
avoid_when: You need complex content workflows, non-JavaScript frontends, comprehensive headless CMS features beyond page building, or deep personalization engines.

auth.notes: API token from Plasmic account. Bearer token auth. Project-scoped access. Webhook for content publish events. REST API for content delivery. React SDK (@plasmicapp/react-web) for visual editor integration. Next.js, Gatsby, and Hydrogen (Shopify) specific adapters. No OAuth2. No fine-grained scopes — API token provides project access.

pricing.paid_tiers: Growth $79/month (5 editors); Scale $299/month (20 editors); Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: San Francisco company. Founded 2018 by Yang Zhang and Chung-Yu Wu. Raised $8M seed (2022). Backed by YCombinator (W20), Andreessen Horowitz (a16z). Figma-to-Plasmic design import is key differentiator (Figma plugin). Visual editor works directly in the browser — no iframe preview gap. React code component integration. Next.js 13+ App Router support. Headless CMS features (not just page builder). 10,000+ projects. Growing competition with Builder.io in the visual CMS space.

performance.rate_limits: API rate limits per plan tier; not publicly documented

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for content delivery. API token auth. Good developer documentation with Next.js integration guides. Webhook for publish events. React SDK required for visual editor integration. Figma-to-Plasmic plugin for design import. YCombinator/a16z backing implies strong developer experience focus. Rate limits not documented publicly.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Page reads fully idempotent. Component reads idempotent. Content queries idempotent by page path. Publish operations idempotent by page ID.
known_agent_gotchas: React SDK required for visual editor to work — REST API alone gives content delivery only
US data residency only — EU customers need GDPR adequacy assessment
Rate limits not documented — test before high-frequency integration
API token provides project-level access — rotate regularly for security
Figma-to-Plasmic import requires Figma plugin installation — separate from API integration

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2 and GDPR compliant. US data residency. API token auth. YCombinator/a16z-backed with startup security practices. API token provides full project access — protect carefully. No OAuth2 or fine-grained scopes. Visual editor runs in browser — page content accessible to authenticated users. Webhook delivery should be verified via signature.
